text: Answer FOUR of the ten essay questions.
There is a limit of 1125 words per essay.

Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write and submit scans or photographs of your answers. Candidates should not copy and paste figures from any other documents.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer FOUR of the ten essays.
content: Compare and contrast the properties of ligand-gated and voltage-gated ion channels.
Discuss ways in which glial cells can influence neuronal signalling at a synapse.
Discuss the factors which might limit the detection of a visual stimulus.
Compare and contrast the encoding of touch and pain.
Compare and contrast the role of the spinal cord and the motor cortex in generating motor outputs.

Intermediate targets (also called 'stepping stone' or 'choice points') are integral to axon pathfinding. Critically discuss. Illustrate your answer with examples of model systems used and mechanisms identified.
Discuss the generation of neuronal diversity along the antero-posterior and dorso-ventral axes in the mammalian hindbrain and spinal cord.
Discuss the roles of Ca²⁺ in short-term and long-term synaptic plasticity.
What does the amygdala contribute to motivated behaviour?
What do the lateral prefrontal and orbitofrontal cortices contribute to cognition?
